top_signals.title: Marcos Says Philippines May Need Extra Budget Amid Oil Shock
top_signals.summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said his government is considering passing a supplemental budget to help the public cope with the oil crisis, as he urged the Senate to return to work.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: U.S. proposes fresh tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor trade practices
top_signals.summary: USTR has proposed a 10% duty rate for economies that have adopted a full or partial prohibition on forced labor trade, and 12.5% for all other economies.

top_signals.title: 100 candles in the wind: Celebrating Marilyn Monroe’s centenary
top_signals.summary: This week marked the 100th anniversary of Marilyn Monroe’s birth. It seems both absurd – she was younger than national treasure Sir David Attenborough – yet also somehow reasonable: she is now a figure from the past, barely even brushed by the outstretched fingertips of memory. Marilyn’s status as an icon is assured, hard-wired into [...]
